headline: Canada Discovers Natural Hydrogen Gas Beneath Mines
meta_description: Scientists in Canada have found that ancient underground rocks naturally produce hydrogen gas, potentially offering a new source of clean energy.
summary_short: Canadian researchers have discovered that ancient underground rocks naturally produce hydrogen gas—a clean energy source that could be abundant and affordable.
body:
Scientists in Canada have made an intriguing discovery: ancient rocks beneath the earth are naturally generating hydrogen gas. This process, which has been happening for millions of years in underground formations, could unlock a new frontier in clean energy production.

The hydrogen being produced through this geological process is called "white hydrogen," and it represents a largely untapped resource. Unlike hydrogen produced through conventional industrial methods, this naturally occurring gas requires no energy input to generate—it forms through natural chemical reactions within the rock layers themselves.

If this source proves abundant and economically viable to extract, it could provide another important tool in the transition to clean energy. The discovery suggests that hydrogen reserves may exist in many locations worldwide, potentially offering communities a new path toward sustainable power.
